content.claim: Hungary's PISA performance profile shows a lower share of top performers (9.3% at Level 5+, vs. OECD 12.6%) and higher share of low performers (28.1% below Level 2, vs. OECD 23.0%), with a third of Roma students in highly segregated school environments
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: PISA cycle(s) from which figures are drawn not specified
Segregation threshold (>25-30% Roma share) is a convention in research literature but not a universal standard
Causality between segregation and performance gaps cannot be inferred from descriptive statistics alone
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
